title: Planet Labs (PL) Stock Surges 10% Following Major European Satellite Imagery Deal
text:
Planet Labs stock surged 10% following news of a major European satellite imagery deal, representing the company's continued success in securing government contracts. The company announced a two-year satellite imagery contract with the Greek government, supported by the European Space Agency, adding to its portfolio of European defense and government customers.

The Greek contract follows other major European deals including a €240 million multi-year German government agreement and a multi-year, low nine-figure agreement with Sweden's Armed Forces. These contracts demonstrate growing acceptance of Planet Labs' satellite imagery and geospatial intelligence services among European defense and security agencies.

Planet Labs' fiscal 2026 results provide strong financial backing for international expansion. The company achieved record revenue of $307.7 million with 26% year-over-year growth, adjusted EBITDA of $15.5 million, and free cash flow of $52.9 million. The $900 million backlog, predominantly from defense and government customers, validates market demand for the company's services.

The company's business model, with 85% of revenue from annual or multi-year contracts and 90% from recurring subscriptions, provides predictability for investors. The Defense & Intelligence segment's 50%+ growth demonstrates accelerating demand. Fiscal 2027 guidance of $415-440 million revenue (35-43% growth) suggests the company will continue to win contracts and pull forward backlog. Each contract win provides validation that government agencies globally are adopting Planet Labs' services.
